title: Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike LIVE: Wangchuk refuses medical intervention at hospital; Dipke holds indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar
content: Even if Sonam can't join the July 20 march to Parliament, I will represent him and lead that march, says wife Gitanjali Angmo, also asking for the activist to be shifted from the Safdarjung Hospital

title: CM Vijay urged to rename Udhagamandalam
content: The popular name of Ootacamund, or Ooty in short, was inexplicably Sanskritised as Udhagamandalam in 1972

title: CJP protesters claim police crackdown, say they are denied access to activist Sonam Wangchuk
content: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke stated that the Delhi police allowed him to reach the protest site only after transferring Mr. Wangchuk to Safdarjung Hospital

title: SDPI alleges Kerala government is complicit in Waqf Board restrictions
content: SDPI accused the government of betraying minority interests by supporting a PIL that led the High Court to impose operational restrictions on the State Waqf Board.

title: Ahead of FIFA World Cup final, Kozhikode police announce public safety measures
content: Kozhikode City police said dangerous bike racing, stunt riding, drunk driving, speeding, traffic violations, bursting of firecrackers in a manner that causes public nuisance, raising provocative or hate-filled slogans, and any other activities that disturb public peace have been prohibited

title: Six resolutions adopted by groups opposing kambala in Mysuru Dasara
content: Participants said that kambala is incompatible with the heritage, cultural identity and religious significance of the world-renowned Dasara festival

title: What makes Skyroot’s Vikram-1 launch unique? | Explained
content: Skyroot’s Vikram-1 has made India only the third country where a private company has independently placed payloads in orbit. Here’s what makes the mission significant

title: Wangchuk’s removal from Jantar Mantar: Congress accuses govt of crushing peaceful protest
content: Attacking the BJP, the Opposition party also said it was a shame that the world's largest democracy was being "ruled" by the most undemocratic and anti-democratic political party in the world

title: Punjab Police arrests inspector linked to U.S. indictment allegations in extortion case
content: On Saturday Deputy Inspector General, Jalandhar Range, Naveen Singla, confirmed the arrest of Inspector Gurinderjit Singh Nagra

title: Supreme Court gives Assam Government two weeks to respond to pleas against foreigner declaration orders
content: A Bench of Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta passed the order after the counsel appearing for the Assam government sought time to file counter-affidavits in the five petitions

title: Supreme Court to hear pleas seeking probe into Ram temple donations theft row on July 20
content: The Bench had also issued notice to the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust seeking its response in the matter

title: Collector warns e-Sevai centres against errors, overcharging
content: The Collector urged the public to use the nearest government e-Sevai centre to avail themselves of government services without unnecessary expenditure of time and money

title: Five persons, including three women drowned in Godavari river while fishing in Polavaram district
content: According to locals, a group of five persons, identified as Lakshmi, Bharathi, Suseela, Balaraju and Ramesh have drowned while they were fishing with the hand-held fishing nets.

title: RJD supremo Lalu Prasad to be taken to AIIMS, Delhi for treatment
content: RJD supremo Lalu Prasad, on July 17, was brought to Patna's IGIMS after experiencing fluctuations in his blood pressure, he will be taken to AIIMS, Delhi for further treatment

title: Contaminated well water sickens more than 80 villagers in Madhya Pradesh's Ratlam; 21 hospitalised, discharged
content: The State Health Department has conducted a door-to-door survey to identify other possible patients and provide timely treatment

title: Erode Corporation revises charges for new underground drainage connections
content: The Corporation said the connection charges cover the cost of providing the sewer connection from the main line up to the property boundary.

title: CJP protest: Woman hurls ink-like liquid at Abhijeet Dipke at Jantar Mantar
content: The woman's identity and the motive behind the act were not immediately known. However, police sources said that she has been detained

title: IMA plans protest against assault on doctors in Dombivli on July 20
content: The IMA has urged government to take strict action against all those involved in the assault, without any political interference and to amend the Maharashtra Medicare Act to prevent violence against healthcare workers

title: Development works on Kalingarayan Canal suspended as water released for irrigation
content: A WRD engineer told The Hindu that around 65% of the works had been completed, while the construction of the baby canal had not yet commenced

title: Coastal lions depend mainly on wild prey, study finds
content: Among the prey species, blue bull (nilgai) was the largest contributor, accounting for 51% of the biomass consumed by lions, wild pigs were the second most important wild prey, while cattle formed the largest domestic prey component

title: Woman assaulted and disrobed on roads in A.P.’s Guntur, accused arrested
content: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu termed the incident “deeply disturbing,” asserting that the humiliation of any woman is unacceptable and assuring that no one would receive protection because of political affiliation

title: Delimitation not just an INDIA bloc issue, concerns our Constitutional framework’ says Congress’ K.C. Venugopal
content: “Delimitation is not a Congress issue or an INDIA bloc issue alone; it is a national issue because it concerns India’s democratic and constitutional framework,” Mr. Venugopal said

title: ‘Still old school, believe in protocol’, says Manish Tewari on buzz around interaction with Modi
content: The remarks came after his interaction with Prime Minister Modi after an official programme in Chandigarh garnered attention on social media

title: Supreme Court allows release of animated film Mahaprabhu Jagannath after Rath Yatra, says animation cannot erode devotion
content: The Bench took the middle path by allowing the movie to be released, but only after the completion of the ongoing Lord Jagannath Rath Yatra on July 27, 2026. The court directed that the film could be released on or after July 28.

title: Brazil-Argentina World Cup rivalry leads to violence in Kerala’s Kozhikode; two booked
content: Argument between Brazil and Argentina supporters in a WhatsApp group over Brazil’s Round of 16 exit escalates into assault and property damage

title: Dr B.R. Ambedkar National Law University holds international seminar
content: Vice-Chancellor Devinder Singh said the rule of law, equal opportunity and inclusive development remained the foundation of democratic societies, and urged students to uphold constitutional values

title: Ram temple donation theft: Police take custody of Tinnu, nephew Manish Yadav
content: On Friday a special anti-corruption court granted a 39-hour police remand of the two key accused, though the police had sought seven days said officials

title: Security tightened at Jantar Mantar, Safdarjung Hospital after Wangchuk shifted amid protest row
content: Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) posted on X that Mr. Wangchuk’s wife, Gitanjali Angmo, was not allowed to carry a phone to meet him in the emergency ward

title: Andhra Pradesh brings in new rules for regulating land use in State
content: The regulations replace multiple legacy zoning frameworks with nine standard land-use categories and adopting a zone-wise negative-list approach

title: K.C. Venugopal urges KSU workers to raise grievances through party forums
content: AICC general secretary says the party 'recognised' that KSU workers might have legitimate concerns but maintained that such issues should first be raised in the appropriate organisational forums

title: Police department sends Kasibugga Sub Inspector on ‘VR’ in case against former Minister’s son
content: The higher officials ordered an internal inquiry and observed that the sub inspector Suneel had not discharged his duty properly and brought bad name to the department

title: Vehicles abandoned on streets of Bengaluru being removed by GBA, BTP
content: On July 18, Greater Bengaluru Development Minister Krishna Byre Gowda flagged off the drive near Malleswaram in the City Central Corporation limits

title: Vikram-1, country’s first private orbital-class rocket, successfully places tech payloads, postcards into orbit
content: Dubbed ‘Mission Aagaman’ (meaning arrival), the successful launch of Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 marks India’s entry into an elite group of nations with private orbital launch capability

title: 2,914 students dropped out of State syllabus schools in 2024-25 in Kerala; 36.65% are migrant workers’ children
content: They discontinued studies either because they, along with their parents, had returned to their native States or their parents had shifted to another location for work, Minister for General Education N. Samsudheen said in a written reply in Assembly recently

title: Gujarat tops NITI Aayog’s first Investment Friendliness Index 2026 among major States
content: Gujarat scored 56.6 points, ahead of Maharashtra (53.7) and Tamil Nadu (53.3), in an assessment of 17 major States

title: M.A. Baby condemns removal of activist Sonam Wangchuk from Jantar Mantar protest site
content: Criticising ‘the authoritarian attitude’ of the Modi government, CPI(M) general secretary M.A. Baby said that ‘silencing dissent cannot be a substitute for accountability’

title: Sharad Pawar slams Centre over ‘irresponsible’ handling of Wangchuk's hunger strike
content: Mr. Pawar asserted that while the government moved to hospitalise Mr. Wangchuk as the situation became "uncontrollable", the protest would continue regardless of the action against the activist

title: Why is ISRO making it harder for scientists to resign? | Explained
content: The Department of Space has tightened resignation rules for scientists working on key missions such as Gaganyaan. Here’s why the government acted, what critics say, and what it means for ISRO

title: CJP’s Abhijeet Dipke begins indefinite hunger strike, as police shifts Wangchuk to hospital
content: Mr. Dipke’s announcement came as the New Delhi DCP’s office said that Mr. Wangchuk has been shifted to the hospital for ‘essential medical care’

title: HYDRAA secures hillocks near Ammuguda, reclaims government land
content: Society to Save Rocks first exposed the destruction of rocks. The Hindu  reported encroachment of land near Ammuguda and destruction of rock formations which were billions of years old

title: Kejriwal slams Centre over Sonam Wangchuk’s hospitalisation, calls it ‘arrogance’
content: Instead of forcibly lifting him (Mr. Wangchuk) up, the Modi government should have talked to Sonam Wangchuk, says AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal

title: Enrol in SIR to get guarantee benefits in Karnataka, says Congress leader
content: Anjali Nimbalkar, former MLA and Congress leader, chaired a meeting at the Belagavi DCC office to review the process of party workers working as BLAs

title: Assembly Deputy Speaker and others meet Pawan
content: Deputy Chief Minister K. Pawan Kalyan receives support from peers after shoulder surgery recovery visit

title: Rescuers race to find survivors in rain after landslip in China kills at least eight
content: The landslide contained about 18,000 cubic meters of rocks and debris, and the largest single rock was around 3,000 cubic meters, Wang Chuanjun, head of Planning and Natural Resources in Pengshui County, told a news conference on July 17

title: Why Kerala’s two private airline projects have hit an air pocket
content: Though Air Kerala and Alhind Air were expected to begin operations last year, uncertainty has derailed their plans, exacerbated by geopolitical tensions and investment woes

title: 23 Andhra villages secure ISO certification for quality citizen services, administration
content: Deputy CM and Minister for Rural Development K. Pawan Kalyan said the international certification was a testament to the good governance in villages

title: ‘Sharad Pawar should take the final call on allying with NDA’
content: Merger unlikely, say leaders from NCP and NCP(SP); Eknath Shinde stays out, as anxiety in NCP(SP) camp grows

title: SIR schedule revised for Maharashtra, extended by 10 days
content: As per the revised schedule, the draft voter list will be published on August 17, and the final list will be published on October 19

title: Investors are returning, says Suvendu; lays foundation stone for ₹15,000-crore steel plant in Bankura
content: Chief Minister promises investor-friendly climate with adequate facilities and conducive law-and-order situation

title: Nothing should be administered to Sonam Wangchuk without my consent, says wife Gitanjali Angmo
content: The Delhi Police has shifted activist Sonam Wangchuk to the Safdarjung Hospital, his wife Gitanjali J Angmo said on Saturday, July 18

title: FIFA weekend puts Hyderabad’s nightlife to test as hospitality industry eyes bigger goal
content: NRAI issues an advisory to restaurants, bars, clubs and microbreweries to ensure the two nights pass without incident

title: Support swells for Sonam Wangchuk’s indefinite fast
content: Several civil society organisations express solidarity with educationist and environmentalist Sonam Wangchuk, who is on an indefinite hunger strike in New Delhi

title: EXIM operations at Kerala’s Vizhinjam seaport set to begin on August 18
content: According to a statement issued by Office of Kerala Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan, commencement of EXIM operations will transform Vizhinjam from a transshipment port into a full-fledged international cargo gateway

title: Bulldozer action on Trinamool MP Abhishek Banerjee's office in Bengal; police deployed
content: A Trinamool Congress party office in Amtala is being demolished by authorities.  District officials state the five-storey building lacked the required building sanction plan.  Notices were issued and a hearing was scheduled, but no response was received.  Bulldozers are bringing down the structure under heavy security escort.  The demolition operation is being supervised by local administrative and police personnel.

title: 'Filthy air': Trump blames Canada as wildfire smoke clouds Argentina vs Spain WC final
content: Wildfire smoke from Canada is impacting air quality in the United States.  This smoke raises concerns for the upcoming FIFA World Cup final match.  Organisers and meteorologists are closely monitoring the air quality forecasts.  Spain's midfielder noted the visible and palpable haze during outdoor training.  FIFA remains optimistic that the championship game will proceed as planned.

title: Goldman Sachs warns staff to donate betting profits for policy violations
content: Goldman Sachs has warned employees against betting on restricted prediction markets.  Staff must limit their activity to sports and entertainment events only.  Violators may forfeit all winnings and face job dismissal for repeated offenses.  This policy aims to prevent insider information conflicts and compliance risks.  Prediction markets face scrutiny after recent incidents involving potential leaks.

title: 'Heartbreaking to see students suffer': Manu Bhaker speaks out on paper leak protests
content: In a post on social media, the Indian shooter said the issue goes beyond politics and called for accountability to protect the future of students.

title: 'Risk of attacks...': Why Germany raised its security alert to 'high threat level'
content: Germany has elevated its national security threat level to high due to intelligence reports.  Increased intelligence indicates clear plans for attacks targeting the country and its people.  The German Cabinet will consider reforms to expand intelligence agency powers soon.  These reforms aim to allow direct intervention in dangerous situations.  Recent deadly attacks have heightened concerns about national security.

title: CJP yet to seek nod for July 20 Parliament march, say Delhi Police sources
content: High political and security drama continues to grip the national capital. The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has not yet approached the Delhi Police to seek official clearance for its high-stakes Parliament march scheduled for July 20, police sources revealed on Saturday.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: Why Marie Curie’s belongings are still kept in lead-lined boxes
content: In a humble Paris shed, Marie and Pierre Curie painstakingly extracted radium from pitchblende, embarking on a journey that would earn them Nobel Prizes and revolutionize cancer treatments. Their meticulous research not only revealed the dangers of radioactive materials, which still pose risks today, but also left a legacy that continues to inspire scientists and medical innovators around the world.

title: US cyclospora outbreak: How the parasite makes people sick
content: The United States is currently grappling with the most significant Cyclospora outbreak in its history, with over one thousand six hundred confirmed cases spanning forty-five states. Health officials are actively tracing potential sources, with lettuce emerging as a major suspect. To mitigate risks, health experts emphasize standard hygiene and food safety practices. This parasite season is expected to last until late August.

title: Scientists fit 75 pronghorns with GPS collars: What they found
content: GPS collars track pronghorn movement near a New Mexico solar project.  Fences around the solar farm present a significant barrier to these animals.  Pronghorn typically crawl under fences rather than jumping over them.  Researchers suggest simple fence modifications could aid wildlife movement.  These changes would help animals use their traditional migration paths.

title: Buying a plot or a house? Here's which loan is right for you
content: Homebuyers are faced with a decision between land loans and home loans when it comes to acquiring property. Home loans cater to those looking to finance ready homes or their construction, while land loans specifically support the purchase of plots. Though home loans provide higher financing limits and longer repayment periods, land loans usually demand larger down payments and may accompany steeper interest rates.

title: Skyroot makes history! 6 payloads put into low-Earth orbit aboard pvt rocket Vikram-1
content: Skyroot Aerospace launched India's first privately developed orbital rocket, Vikram-1.  The vehicle successfully placed four technology demonstration satellites into low-Earth orbit.  This launch marks a significant milestone in opening India's space sector to private companies.  Vikram-1 utilized advanced carbon-composite structures and a restartable liquid-fuelled module.  The mission validates India's capabilities in private orbital launch vehicle development.

title: 'Messi a source of pride for La Masia': Barcelona makes big statement ahead of final
content: Laporta is in New York ahead of Sunday's final at New York/New Jersey Stadium, where current and former Barcelona players will feature on both sides. Spain's squad includes eight Barcelona players, while Messi will captain Argentina.

title: PV Sindhu breaks Chen Yufei jinx, becomes first Indian woman to ...
content: PV Sindhu has reached the Japan Open 2026 final, a historic achievement for Indian badminton. She advanced after her opponent Chen Yufei retired due to injury during their match. Sindhu secured her first win against Chen since 2019, ending a significant losing streak. This marks her return to a BWF Tour title match after over two years. She will now face Akane Yamaguchi for the championship title.

title: New money? India's currency might soon get a plastic makeover
content: India's Reserve Bank is exploring polymer banknotes for enhanced durability. Manufacturers are invited to bid for specialized plastic substrate materials. Polymer notes offer greater resistance to moisture and dirt than paper. This move aims to reduce currency replacement and printing costs. Advanced security features will also be incorporated into the new notes.

title: 'He was everything we dream of becoming': Gavaskar's moving tribute to Garry Sobers
content: Widely regarded as cricket's greatest all-rounder, Sobers died at his home in Barbados, 11 days before his 90th birthday. During a career spanning two decades, he set new standards with his batting, bowling and fielding.

title: Plain clothes, white sheets, 3 layers: How Delhi Police planned Wangchuk's shift to hospital
content: Delhi Police conducted a pre-planned operation to remove Sonam Wangchuk from Jantar Mantar.  The activist was then shifted to Safdarjung Hospital due to his health.  Police cited a Delhi High Court order and medical advice for the action.  Protesters continued their demonstration after Wangchuk's removal from the site.  The operation aimed to minimize resistance and ensure a clear route.

title: 'It's just insane': Messi reacts to viral photo with baby Yamal ahead of final
content: "That picture with Lamine, now we are facing in a final after a photo was taken of when he was a baby," Messi told seven-time Super Bowl winner Tom Brady at Fanatics Fest on Friday. "It's just insane."

title: Ranbir Kapoor, Sai Pallavi spotted at airport ahead of 'Ramayana' event
content: The promotional campaign for Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana has officially begun today. Actors Ranbir Kapoor and Sai Pallavi departed for New Delhi for the launch event. The grand event will feature an exclusive preview of the film's first trailer. This ambitious mythological spectacle is set for a Diwali 2026 release. The team will also promote the film at Comic-Con and TIFF.

title: Wangchuk opposed hospitalisation a day before; CJP shares video
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk opposed his hospitalization, stating his fast was self-chosen.  Delhi Police moved him to Safdarjung Hospital citing health concerns and court orders.  The Cockroach Janta Party shared a video showing Wangchuk's objection to hospitalization efforts.  Wangchuk has been on an indefinite hunger strike since June 28 supporting the NEET protest.  His wife also questioned the decision to hospitalize him without consent.

title: After 'Dhurandhar', Aditya Dhar eyes grand Lachit Borphukan biopic
content: Aditya Dhar could soon be taking on another high-profile project. Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has revealed that he has approached the director to helm a biopic on legendary Ahom commander Lachit Borphukan.

title: 'Cricket has lost one of its greatest': Virat Kohli mourns Garry Sobers' death
content: Kohli took to X to express his condolences and praised Sobers' impact on the game. "Cricket has lost one of its greatest. Rest in peace, Sir Garfield Sobers. Your legacy will inspire generations," Kohli wrote.

title: 'Just like Messi for Argentina': Kaif makes strong case for Rohit, Kohli in 2027 WC
content: Mohammed Kaif believes Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli are essential for India's 2027 World Cup campaign. He stressed their experience will be vital for challenging conditions in South Africa. Kaif compared their importance to Lionel Messi's impact on his football team. Experienced players offer guidance and create opportunities for younger teammates. India will need these seasoned cricketers under captain Shubman Gill.

title: Blood oozing from nose, UP cop found dead inside car
content: A sub-inspector was found dead inside his locked car in Para on Friday.  He had been out of contact with colleagues since Thursday afternoon.  Police traced his mobile phone to the Budheshwar-Mohan road location.  Doctors declared him dead after he was shifted to a hospital.  Forensic examination and post-mortem will establish the exact cause of death.

title: CDC finds shredded lettuce behind Taco Bell-linked cyclospora outbreak
content: US health officials identified Taco Bell's shredded lettuce as the source of a Cyclospora outbreak. This parasitic infection sickened over 1,600 people and led to 94 hospitalizations across five states. Investigations traced the contaminated lettuce through the supply chain to a farm in central Mexico. Taylor Farms voluntarily removed its iceberg lettuce from the US market and initiated a recall.

title: 'They rigged election, & I got FIFA WC': Trump praises Balogun decision, repeats 2020 claim
content: Trump made the remarks during a reception at Trump Tower before a FIFA Council meeting on Friday, two days ahead of Sunday's World Cup final.

title: Eye on Russia: UK's Storm Fighter, Germany’s Tranche‑4 Eurofighters signal new era of air defence
content: Europe is increasing defense spending to counter Russian aggression and enhance collective security.  The United Kingdom is developing its Storm Fighter drone program for future combat operations. Germany is modernizing its air force by fielding new Tranche 4 Eurofighter aircraft. These initiatives reflect a broader European push for military capability upgrades.  Nations are investing in autonomous systems and updating existing fighter fleets.

title: Shatrughan Sinha backs Sonakshi Sinha for supporting Sonam Wangchuk, his team thanks her
content: Veteran actor and politician Shatrughan Sinha has publicly praised daughter Sonakshi Sinha for speaking in support of activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on an indefinite hunger strike at Delhi's Jantar Mantar over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak issue. Following the support from Sonakshi Sinha and actor Omi Vaidya, Sonam Wangchuk's team responded on behalf of the activist, expressing gratitude for their solidarity.

title: 'Promoting love jihad': Aamir Khan gets 'choke the breath' threat from Lawrence Bishnoi gang
content: The Lawrence Bishnoi gang has issued death threats against Bollywood actor Aamir Khan. Gangsters Aarzoo Bishnoi and Tyson Bishnoi made the threats via social media and audio clips. The group also warned politicians shielding alleged culprits in a Rajasthan case. They paid tribute to deceased associates, vowing retaliation for their sacrifice. The gang claims Khan and others promote 'love jihad' against their culture.

title: Sonam Wangchuk Hospitalised Live Updates: Sonam Wangchuk's Wife Appeals To Shift Him To Another Hospital For Treatment
content: Sonam Wangchuk Hospitalised Live Updates: The action followed a Delhi High Court order for daily health checks on Sonam Wangchuk due to his deteriorating condition.

title: Skyroot's Vikram-1 Launch Live Updates: "Grand Success," Says Skyroot As Vikram-1 Successfully Releases Payload In Space
content: Skyroot Vikram-1 Launch, Mission Aagaman Live News Updates: The launch is seen as a major milestone for India's rapidly expanding private space sector.

title: Toxic Gas, Bomb Tests: Jaish Terrorists Arrested In Gujarat Took Special Training In J&K
content: The suspects were taught a total of 40 distinct terror techniques, ranging from precision bomb-making to the synthesis of toxic gases using everyday organic materials left underground to ferment.

title: On Camera, Crocodile Drags 12-Year-Old UP Boy Into River, Kills Him In Front Of Uncle
content: The incident reportedly took place on Thursday evening in Muraunwa village in the Baundi police station area, Bahraich.

title: "Those With One Marriage Will Stay In Madhya Pradesh": Mohan Yadav On Uniform Civil Code Bill
content: Madhya Pradesh government to propose Uniform Civil Code bill ensuring legal recognition of only one marriage.

title: Opinion: Reporter's Diary: How Sonam Wangchuk Was Removed From Protest Site
content: There was a sense of uncertainty among the protesters, who appeared anxious but were unaware of what was about to happen.

title: "He Is Still On Hunger Strike": Sonam Wangchuk's Wife To NDTV After His Hospitalisation
content: Sonam Wangchuk, who had been on a hunger strike demanding the resignation of the education minister over an alleged NEET paper leak, appeared fine on Friday, his wife said.

title: Markets Regulator Warns Of "Boss Scam", Cyber Fraud Targeting Top Executives
content: The regulator said it issued the advisory after receiving information from the Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre (I4C) about a rise in such cases.

title: Delhi-Rishikesh Namo Bharat: How Rail Project Will Transform Two States
content: The Centre has given in-principle approval to extend Delhi-Meerut Regional Rapid Transit System from Modipuram in Meerut to Haridwar and Rishikesh.

title: Infiltration Bid Foiled In J&K, Heavy Firing Along LoC In Rajouri
content: This marks the first major ceasefire violation along the LoC since Operation Sindoor. Before this, only minor incidents involving eight to 10 rounds of firing were reported.

title: Passkeys, Recovery Email: Simple Steps To Secure Your WhatsApp Account
content: Meta has warned that scammers are trying to trick users into linking their WhatsApp account to another device.

title: "Zero Tolerance": Nara Lokesh As TDP Worker Arrested For Assaulting Woman
content: Andhra Pradesh Education and IT Minister Nara Lokesh said the government would not tolerate crimes against women, irrespective of the accused's political background.

title: "Proved My Faith In India's Youth Right": PM Modi Dials Skyroot Team After Historic Launch
content: Commending the young Skyroot team, the Prime Minister said they had shown what India's youth are capable of

title: Hello Space, Vikram-1 Has Arrived: Skyroot Scripts India's Historic Rocket Mission
content: The mission, called Aagaman, marks the arrival of India's private sector in the global launch business.

title: India's 1st Geothermal Wells Commissioned At 14,000 Feet In Ladakh
content: Established at an altitude of 14,000 feet, the two deep wells are expected to pave the way for a one-megawatt geothermal power plant in Ladakh.

title: "3...2...1...Lift Off": Moment India's 1st Private Space Rocket Took Off. Watch
content: Vikram-1 is India's longest monolithic carbon composite rocket stage, which showcases advances in indigenous composite manufacturing and aerospace engineering.

title: Punjab Cop Who Extorted Rs 4 Crore From Family And Was Wanted By US Arrested
content: Punjab police officer Gurinderjit Singh Nagra arrested over US racketeering and extortion allegations.

title: Mars, Moon, Sun, Sea: India's Space Achievements As Vikram-1 Lifts Off
content: Skyroot Aerospace's plans to launch the country's first privately developed orbital-class rocket before August 4 mark the next major step for the growing Indian space industry.

content: Responding to the claim, the US Central Command (CENTCOM) said, "Like most IRGC claims, this is false," on X.

title: Aamir Khan receives alleged death threat from Lawrence Bishnoi gang
content: The TOI Entertainment Desk is a dynamic and dedicated team of journalists, working tirelessly to bring the pulse of the entertainment world straight to the readers of The Times of India. No red carpet goes unrolled, no stage goes dark - our team spans the globe, bringing you the latest scoops and insider insights from Bollywood to Hollywood, and every entertainment hotspot in between. We don't just report; we tell tales of stardom and stories untold. Whether it's the rise of a new sensation or the seasoned journey of an industry veteran, the TOI Entertainment Desk is your front-row seat to the fascinating narratives that shape the entertainment landscape.
